title: Grabowski soll den feingranularen Bureau-Lease-Vertrag erzwingen
goal: Grabowski consumes Bureau's machine-readable lease contract v2, rejects the broad Bureau repository key for normal work, accepts exact object/component scopes, and serializes only short worktree-admin, merge and emergency-recovery effects.

acceptance.assertion: Grabowski evaluates the deployed Bureau lease-contract v2 and fails closed for unknown operations, invalid output or unavailable contract evidence.
Normal Bureau analysis, implementation, Registry work and merge attempts that request repo:/home/alex/repos/bureau are rejected before acquisition.
Different task, initiative and resource files plus declared component scopes can be acquired independently even while unrelated Bureau work is active.
Linked-worktree administration and main merge require their dedicated gates with an explicit TTL of at most 300 seconds and release immediately after the effect.
The broad Bureau repository key is accepted only for explicitly justified emergency recovery with an expected state or head and a TTL of at most 300 seconds.
Live Register reads and appends remain usable without acquiring or overriding any Git work lease.
The merged Grabowski runtime is deployed and a live smoke proves broad-key refusal, parallel exact scopes, short gates and always-on operational state.

metadata.registration_boundary: This task does not alter the active foreign Grabowski ball, grant merge/deploy authority, or weaken non-Bureau repository leases.
metadata.supersedes_semantics: Replaces the v1 rule that a foreign broad Bureau repo lease blocks reviewed Git Registry mutations; v2 uses exact object/component scopes and forbids the broad key for normal work.
metadata.verification_note: Grabowski PR #169 merged with green Python 3.10/3.12 checks. The deployed runtime is a 29-commit descendant and live-enforces the Bureau v2 contract.
